column1: がんで死亡する (しぼうする)
〜とおり(に) / 〜どおり(に) (just as / exactly as)
column2:
Doing something exactly as seen, heard, or thought.
Connection: Vる / Vた / Nの + とおり / どおり
Ex: 友達が言ったとおり、日本の物価は高い。 — Just as my friend said, prices in Japan are expensive.
自分の思うとおりに生きることは難しい。 — It is difficult to live exactly as one thinks/wishes.

Doing something following one's feelings or external instructions without changing anything.
Connection: Vる / Vた / Nの + まま(に)
Ex: セールスマンに言われるまま、契約書にサインをしてしまった。 — I signed the contract just as the salesman told me to (without thinking).
足の向くままに歩く。 — Walk wherever my feet take me (aimlessly).

Used to express the speaker's emotional reaction to a fact.
Connection: Vた / Aい / naな + ことに
Ex: 驚いたことに、彼女は同じ相手と2回も離婚して再婚しているんです。 — What surprised me was that she divorced and remarried the same person twice.
おもしろいことに、隣の家も、その隣の家も、うちと同じ名字なのです。 — Interestingly, both the neighbor next door and their neighbor have the same surname as us.

column1: 〜あまり(に) (so... that / because of too much...)
column2:
Because of an excessive degree of feeling or state, a result occurred.
Connection: Nの / Vる / naな / Aい + あまり(に)
Ex: 緊張のあまり、体が震えた。 — I was so nervous that my body was shaking.
心配するあまり、母は寝込んでしまった。 — My mother was so worried she fell ill.

Explaining the reason for a situation; "naturally so."
Connection: V / A / na / N + わけだ
Ex: 寒いわけだ。雪が降っている。(寒いのは当然だ) — No wonder it's cold. It's snowing.
定価が1万円で、2割引きだから8千円になるわけだ。(結局8千円になる) — The price is 10,000 yen, and with a 20% discount, it naturally comes to 8,000 yen.

column1: 〜わけではない / 〜わけでもない (it is not that / it doesn't mean)
column2:
Partially denying a situation; "not necessarily so."
Connection: V / A / na / N + わけではない
Ex: ほしくないわけではないけれど、お金がないから買えないんです。(ほしいですが) — It's not that I don't want it, I just can't afford it.
テレビはつけてあるが、見ているわけでもない。(特に見ていない) — The TV is on, but it doesn't mean I'm watching it.

column1: 〜わけがない (there is no way that)
column2:
Strong negative judgment; "absolutely not."
Connection: V / A / na / N + わけがない
Ex: うちの子がそんなことをするわけがない。(絶対にしない) — There is no way my child would do such a thing.
ほめられて、うれしくないわけがない。(絶対にうれしい) — How could I not be happy to be praised?

column1: 〜わけにはいかない / 〜わけにもいかない (cannot afford to / cannot help but)
column2:
Cannot do something due to social/moral obligations or circumstances.
Connection: Vる / Vない + わけにはいかない
Ex: 重要な会議があるので、病気でも会社を休むわけにはいかない。 — Because of an important meeting, I cannot take a day off even if I am sick.
税金は、高くても払わないわけにはいかない。 — No matter how high taxes are, I have to pay them.

column1: 〜とたん(に) (the moment / as soon as)
column2:
An unexpected or immediate change following an action.
Connection: Vた + とたん(に)
Ex: 窓を開けたとたん、強い風が入ってきた。 — The moment I opened the window, a strong wind came in.
お酒を飲んだとたん、顔が赤くなった。 — As soon as I drank the alcohol, my face turned red.

column1: 〜あげく(に) (in the end / after a long...)
column2:
After a long process of difficulty/action, a negative result occurs.
Connection: Vた / Nの + あげく(に)
Ex: 彼は酔っ払って騒いだあげく、寝てしまった。 — He got drunk and made a ruckus, and in the end, he just fell asleep.
何度も手術したあげく、治らないと言われた。 — After undergoing surgery many times, I was told it wouldn't cure me.

After much effort or a long period of thinking/waiting, a final result is reached.
Connection: Vた / Nの + 末(に)
Ex: 悩んだ末、進学ではなく就職することにした。 — After much thought, I decided to get a job instead of continuing my studies.
住民との話し合いの末、建設は中止となった。 — After discussions with the residents, construction was cancelled.

column1: 〜かと思ったら (just when I thought...)
column2:
X happened, and immediately or unexpectedly, Y happened.
Connection: Vた + か + Vた + かの + と思ったら
Ex: あの人は忙しい人で、来たかと思ったら、もう帰ってしまった。 — That person is so busy; just when I thought he had arrived, he had already left.
花が咲いたかと思ったら、もう散ってしまった。 — Just when I thought the flowers had bloomed, they had already withered.

Specifies a moment or situation.
Connection: Vる / Vた / Vている / Aい + ところ
Ex: お忙しいところをすみません。 — Sorry to bother you when you are busy.
会いたいと思っていたところです。 — I was just thinking about wanting to see you.

When I tried [X], I found [Y] (discovery).
Connection: Vた + ところ
Ex: 病院で検査したところ、異常はなかった。 — When I got examined at the hospital, there were no problems.
駅に問い合わせたところ、忘れ物は届いていた。 — When I inquired at the station, the lost item had been turned in.

column1: 〜どころではない / 〜どころじゃない (not the time for / far from)
column2:
Not in a situation or state to do something.
Connection: Vる / N + どころではない
Ex: 工場で火事があり、仕事どころではなかった。 — Because of the fire at the factory, I wasn't in a state to work.
風邪がひどくて、遊びに行くどころじゃない。 — My cold is so bad, I'm in no state to go out and play.

column1: 〜どころか (let alone / far from being)
column2:
Expresses the opposite of what is expected (e.g., "Not only did I not get X, I even got Y [or didn't get Y]").
Connection: V / A / na / N + どころか
Ex: 忙しくて夏休みどころか日曜日も休めないよ。 — I'm so busy I can't even take Sundays off, let alone a summer vacation.
うちの会社は忙しいどころか仕事がなくて困っているんだ。 — My company isn't even busy; we don't have enough work.

Covered in something (usually something undesirable).
Connection: N + だらけ
Ex: この手紙の日本語は間違いだらけだ。 — The Japanese in this letter is full of mistakes.
ちゃんとたたんでおかなかったから、服がしわだらけになってしまった。 — I didn't fold it properly, so my clothes are full of wrinkles.

Doing something and not changing the state; or "only once/one person."
Connection: Vた / N + きり
Ex: うちの子は遊びに行ったきり帰ってこない。 — My child went out to play and hasn't returned since (has stayed out).
息子は、自分の部屋に入ったきり出てこない。 — My son went into his room and hasn't come out since.
彼とは去年の忘年会に会って、それっきり会っていない。 — I met him at the year-end party last year, and haven't met since.

column1: 〜っぱなし (leaving something in a state)
column2:
Leaving an action in a state of completion (usually with a negative nuance of neglect).
Connection: Vます + っぱなし
Ex: 電車が混んでいて、東京まで立ちっぱなしだった。 — The train was crowded, so I had to stand the whole way to Tokyo.
テレビをつけっぱなしで寝てしまった。 — I fell asleep leaving the TV on.

Indicates a continuous trend in one direction (usually negative or passive changes).
Connection: Vる + 一方だ
Ex: 大気中の濃度の上昇とともに、地球の温暖化は進む一方だ。 — Along with the rise in CO2, global warming just keeps progressing.
天候のせいで、野菜の値段は上がる一方だ。 — Due to the weather, vegetable prices just keep going up.

Opposed to a prediction, desire, or expectation.
Connection: N + に反して
Ex: 専門家の予想に反して、景気は回復し始めた。 — Contrary to experts' predictions, the economy has started to recover.
我々の期待に反して、新商品はあまり売れなかった。 — Contrary to our expectations, the new product did not sell well.

column1: 〜反面 / 〜半面 (on the other hand / while)
column2:
Expresses two conflicting sides of the same thing.
Connection: V / A / na / N + 反面 / 半面
Ex: パソコンは便利な反面、トラブルも多い。 — PCs are convenient, but on the other hand, they have many problems.
彼は優れた学者である反面、精神的に弱い人間だ。 — He is an excellent scholar, but on the other hand, he is mentally fragile.

Contrasting two situations or showing two roles/aspects.
Connection: V / A / na / N + 一方(で)
Ex: この布は水に強い一方、熱に弱い。 — This cloth is strong against water, but on the other hand, it is weak against heat.
予算がないと言われているが、一方では予算がむだに使われている。 — It is said there is no budget, but on the other hand, it is being wasted.
keeps continuing in one direction (a one-way trend, often worsening)
is in the process of happening / is currently underway
to reach the point of / to result in (something serious)
that is the reason why / it depends entirely on
ended up in (a bad, regrettable state)
someone else's problem / not one's own concern

column1: しつけがなっていない
column2: poorly brought up / lacking basic discipline
to fold up (e.g. a stroller) to take up less space
dangerous / unsettling (of a neighborhood or situation)
proverb: "even a thief has his reasons" — everyone has some justification
to not treat (someone) as a human being
to think only of efficiency, disregarding other people
to spend time idly / aimlessly, without purpose
peculiar to / characteristic of (a particular stage or thing)
to regard (something) as special or different from the rest
to fritter away one's time playing, to excess
the separation of smoking and non-smoking areas
no smoking all day / all-day smoking ban
to argue back / to counter an objection
the effect that something has on the people around it
is not necessarily the case that...
is nothing more than... / is merely...
must be the case / no doubt that...
could well happen / runs the risk of (something bad)
bumpy / uneven (a surface with high and low points)
smooth / perfectly even (no bumps at all)
the fee paid for school lunches
gratitude for (the taking of) life — the meaning behind saying いただきます
to treat pragmatically / to accept something without overthinking it
memory capacity / the ability to remember
to place heavy importance on rote memorization
to be presumptuous / to overstep one's proper place
